### Post by shunt31 on 2010-05-28
I have my Aiptek 6000 (not USB) connected to the serial port on the computer. It doesn't show up in /dev/event, although I have the xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen and xserver-xorg-input-aiptek drivers installed.
My xorg.conf is: 
```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder75)  Fri Mar 12 01:42:27 PST 2010


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    InputDevice    "tablet"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "HyperPen"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "tablet"
    Driver         "HyperPen"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/ttyS0"
    Option         "Mode" "absolute"
    Option         "Cursor" "Stylus"
    Option         "XSize" "600"
    Option         "YSize" "450"
    Option         "AlwaysCore"
    Option         "BaudRate" "19200"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1400x1900"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Changing "Device" "/dev/ttyS0" to ttyS1,2 or 3 doesn't help.

What would I do with the driver at [http://sourceforge.net/projects/xf86digitaledge/?](http://sourceforge.net/projects/xf86digitaledge/?) Makes throws lots of errors

### Post by Favux on 2010-05-28
Hi shunt31,

Welcome to Ubuntu forums!

The Aiptek driver is for usb tablets.  The XF86 DigitalEdge XInput driver clearly hasn't been maintained in a long time and won't build on the Xserver you have.

So what release of Ubuntu are you in?  Is the Aiptek 6000 also called the Aiptek HyperPen?

Do you see anything with:
```
xinput --list
```
or
```
dmesg | grep ttyS
```
Since you are using the xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen, if the tablet is showing up in xinput, we should see if the driver is throwing errors on boot by looking at Xorg.0.log in /var/logs.  You can compress it with right click Create Archive and attach it to your post with Manage Attachments below.

---

my tablet.

### Post by Favux on 2010-05-29
Hi paganx,

Welcome to the thread.

I did a little investigating and it looks like Intrepid was the last release with the serial hyperpen driver.  That was the 1.2.0 version:  [http://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen](http://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen)  It was updated to 1.3.0 on 2-26-09 by Peter Hutterer:  [http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/](http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/)  That version may have worked with Jaunty and possibly Karmic, even though Ubuntu didn't include it in the repositories.  The last activity was again by Peter on 7-17-09.  So it looks like the only current Ubuntu release that supports HyperPen is Hardy.

Debian lenny has 1.2.0:  [http://packages.debian.org/lenny/xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen](http://packages.debian.org/lenny/xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen)

The maintainer appears to be the Debian X strike force:  [http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce)  But they seem to have abandoned supporting it.  Can follow up on the three individuals named.  If they are with Debian it's possible they have an updated git.

Could beg Peter to update it for the Xserver 1.7 in Lucid.  He's a Red Hat Xorg dev. and so could do it.  However he seems very busy with one of his latest projects being the Xorg Wacom X driver xf86-input-wacom over the last several months, which is now the X driver in Lucid/Xserver 1.7.

Another alternative might be repurposing another serial tablet driver for the HyperPen.  Do you know of any?  The only one I know of is for xf86-input-wacom.  It actually dropped serial support but there are some new patches submitted that restore serial support.

Probably should submit bug reports to Ubuntu's launchpad, Debian, and maybe the X stike force.

### Post by Favux on 2010-05-29
Hi paganx,

The bug you are reporting is that 1.3.0 doesn't build on Xserver 1.7 and the driver isn't in the repositories, ie your tablet doesn't work in Lucid.  A newbie can open a bug report.  Then you want Shunt31 to join in and confirm the bug/report so be sure to link to your bug reports on this thread and your other HperPen thread.  You're right the more the better.  But a report has to be started before others can join in.

Good luck!

### Post by firefox791 on 2010-06-03
Hi!

I recently had the same trouble and contacted one of the maintainers. He was able to get me up and running once (only a little confusion for me because of the signing - you have to use your own key).
However, even more recently than that I have reinstalled ubuntu 10.04 (because I banned windows), and I've tried to reproduce it, but it didn't work at all. Here's my story (in case anyone else gets lucky as I did on my first try, or in case anyone can help me)

Here's the EMail:

[INDENT]
The driver was removed from the archive during karmic, so it's not available anymore. The latest upstream version is here:

[http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/](http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/)

you can build it like this:

```

git clone git://anongit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen
cd xf86-input-hyperpen
<copy the debian directory from the old source package here>
debuild -b

```

or just grab the source package from Debian (1.3.0-1) and apply this patch on top of it:

[http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/patch/?id=7f3815f74e77df6122320d845d6e7e9541a28b76](http://cgit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen/patch/?id=7f3815f74e77df6122320d845d6e7e9541a28b76)

and then debuild -b.
[/INDENT]

**Heres what I did:**

1. get the dependencies and tools: (EDIT - added fakeroot and build-essential)
```

sudo apt-get install git-core bzr devscripts debhelper xserver-xorg-dev x11proto-randr-dev fakeroot build-essential

```

2. get the two repositories:
```

git clone git://anongit.freedesktop.org/xorg/driver/xf86-input-hyperpen
bzr branch lp:ubuntu/jaunty/xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen

```

3. copy the debian folder
```

cp -R xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen/debian xf86-input-hyperpen/debian

```

4. create keys (Applications - Accessories - Passwords and Encryption Keys -- File - New - PGP Key) and remember the KeyID (Its in the Personal Keys tab)

5. change to the directory and debuild it (EDIT - added sudo)
```

cd xf86-input-hyperpen
sudo debuild -b -rfakeroot -k<Key ID>

```

when I first did this, there was no problem at all, and the deb was there and could be installed.

6. Adapt your xorg.conf. This was the hardest part. I ended up using parts of different versions I found:
[INDENT]
1. the one mentioned  [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=69355") 
2. the one mentioned [here]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu:Lucid#xorg.conf_for_nvidia")
3. Most of the one i already had, after installing the restricted nvidia driver.
[/INDENT]

the fourth ingredient is a lot of patience. (ALWAYS back up!)

That worked for me, so if it helps you, congratulations! 

**I got a lot of problems this time though:**

```

elrond@RIVENDELL:~/xf86-input-hyperpen$ sudo debuild -b -rfakeroot -k********
 dpkg-buildpackage -rfakeroot -D -us -uc -b
dpkg-buildpackage: warning: using a gain-root-command while being root
dpkg-buildpackage: set CFLAGS to default value: -g -O2
dpkg-buildpackage: set CPPFLAGS to default value: 
dpkg-buildpackage: set LDFLAGS to default value: -Wl,-Bsymbolic-functions
dpkg-buildpackage: set FFLAGS to default value: -g -O2
dpkg-buildpackage: set CXXFLAGS to default value: -g -O2
dpkg-buildpackage: source package xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen
dpkg-buildpackage: source version 1:1.2.0-1build2
dpkg-buildpackage: source changed by Timo Aaltonen <tjaalton@ubuntu.com>
dpkg-buildpackage: host architecture i386
 fakeroot debian/rules clean
dh_testdir
dh_testroot
rm -f build-stamp
rm -f config.cache config.log config.status
rm -f */config.cache */config.log */config.status
rm -f conftest* */conftest*
rm -rf autom4te.cache */autom4te.cache
rm -rf obj-*
dh_clean
 debian/rules build
dh_testdir
test -d obj-i486-linux-gnu || mkdir obj-i486-linux-gnu
cd obj-i486-linux-gnu && \
	../configure --prefix=/usr --mandir=\${prefix}/share/man \
	             --infodir=\${prefix}/share/info --build=i486-linux-gnu --disable-static \
	             CFLAGS="-Wall -g -O2" 
configure: WARNING: unrecognized options: --disable-static
configure: error: cannot find install-sh, install.sh, or shtool in . ".."/.
make: *** [build-stamp] Error 1
dpkg-buildpackage: error: debian/rules build gave error exit status 2
debuild: fatal error at line 1340:
dpkg-buildpackage -rfakeroot -D -us -uc -b failed
elrond@RIVENDELL:~/xf86-input-hyperpen$ 

```

I'd appreciate it if anyone can help!

### Post by firefox791 on 2010-08-20
Seeing as this tablet has THREE connectors (PS2, Serial and Another I can't remember the name for atm) I'd hardly think that an Adapter would work. Also, I'm quite sure the USB tablet uses a different protocol.

Another problem is, my tablet's broken :( (Actually I suspect it's the pen that's causing problems)

I'll give the Compiling a new try though. I'm on my laptop, so maybe new machine = new luck. It doesn't have a serial port...

Ok, I got the error "Configure not found". I actually went and checked in the xf86-input-hyperpen folder and it didn't have a configure. There was makefile.am, configure.ac and autogen.sh though. I tried running autogen.sh and lo and behold - I didn't have autoconf... Talk about having all requirements.

Having installed the autoconf packaage, I ran debuild again with all the options and got

```

configure: WARNING: unrecognized options: --disable-static
configure: error: cannot find install-sh, install.sh, or shtool in . ".."/.

```

The first one is just a warning so I'll let it be for now, but the even after "sudo apt-get install"ing shtool I got the same error. I ran autogen.sh manually, and found out libtool was missing. I ran it again, and this time it seemed to terminate without problems. It even ran configure for me and spat out a makefile. So:

```

make

```

This didn't seem to get very far because some Defines were missing... So I gedit-ed config.h and added them:

```

#define PACKAGE_VERSION_MAJOR 1
#define PACKAGE_VERSION_MINOR 3
#define PACKAGE_VERSION_PATCHLEVEL 0

```

after

```

/* Define to the version of this package. */
#define PACKAGE_VERSION "1.3.0"

```

making afterwards works. I tried debuild again and this time I got further: 

```

dh_install: xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen missing files (usr/lib/xorg/modules/input/*.so), aborting
make: *** [binary-arch] Error 255
dpkg-buildpackage: error: fakeroot debian/rules binary gave error exit status 2
debuild: fatal error at line 1340:
dpkg-buildpackage -rfakeroot -D -us -uc -b failed

```

It didn't find any shared objects. Looking at the make output, it never made anything either...

I couldn't get any further than this right now, I might look a bit further later. Anyone notice anything wrong or got any further yet?

### Post by firefox791 on 2010-10-30
I recently retried this in Jaunty, and it worked. I packed everything into a folder and wrote Myself a README, which includes getting MyPaint 0.8.2 to work too (since that's what I use my tablet for):

1. Install Dependencies for MyPaint and Driver

```

apt-get install scons python-numpy python-dev protobuf-compiler swig libglib2.0-dev python-protobuf autoconf automake libtool shtool git-core devscripts debhelper xserver-xorg-dev x11proto-randr-dev fakeroot build-essential

```

Note: might need to add jaunty-backports to sources.list.
Also: get bzr_2.0.0-0ubuntu1_i386 from [http://packages.ubuntu.com](http://packages.ubuntu.com)

2. Follow tutorial for installing xserver-xorg-input-hyperpen provided, with following modifications:

run ./autogen.sh in xf86-input-hyperpen

add to xf86-input-hyperpen/src/xf86HyperPen.c

```

#define PACKAGE_VERSION_MAJOR 1
#define PACKAGE_VERSION_MINOR 2
#define PACKAGE_VERSION_PATCHLEVEL 0

```

before running debuild. Then install the deb.

3. Modify the xorg.conf (Mine looked like this, after I added the InputDevice and ServerLayout sections):

```

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier "pen"
    Driver     "hyperpen"
    Option     "Device"         "/dev/ttyS0"
    Option "Mode" "absolute"
    Option "Cursor" "Stylus"
    Option "SendCoreEvents"
    Option "XSize" "600"
    Option "YSize" "450"
    Option "AlwaysCore"
    Option "BaudRate" "19200"
    Option "PMin" "62" 
    Option "PMax" "511"
 EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	InputDevice	"pen" "SendCoreEvents"
	Screen	"Default Screen"
EndSection

```

Relog after that.

4. Lastly, Mypaint should be easy:

```

install mypaint:
scons and then run ./mypaint

```

Hope this helps! I'll try to adapt it to maverick or lucid soon.
